story.text: As bdo, I need a per-file mask in my VS Code viewport showing which files agents have NOT discovered yet (undiscovered), alongside edited/red which VS Code paints natively, so I can see at a glance where attention has and has not reached across the whole tree — because I asked for exactly that file-mask (discovered / red / edited). This step is the SENSOR plus a coverage fold: record which files each session reads/edits so 'discovered vs undiscovered' becomes computable data the step-2 extension can paint.

briefing.headline: A file-touch sensor + discovered-coverage fold for the per-file mask
briefing.value: The forest fold (0186) and the live workspace (0188) make the whole tree of work navigable; this adds the per-FILE layer bdo asked for. A PostToolUse sensor (.claude/hooks/file_touch.py) records every Read/Edit as one {ts, session, action, path} record, and loop/coverage.py folds those into the set of DISCOVERED repo-relative paths (ever read or edited) vs UNDISCOVERED tracked files (never touched), with per-directory counts and a --json dataset the step-2 VS Code extension consumes. It is the activity-accounting runtime witness (organ 2, loop/activity.py done-line 0143) turned on the file tools, declared in activity-register.json so the §10 teeth pass.
briefing.why_now: bdo asked for the file-mask this session. 'Discovered vs undiscovered' is not data the harness collects yet — edited/red VS Code already paints, but no record says which files an agent's attention ever reached. Without the sensor first, the step-2 mask has nothing to paint. This build is exactly the sensor + the fold, no further.
briefing.if_accepted: Every session's file reads/edits land on .ai-native/log/file-touch.jsonl (gitignored telemetry), and `python -m loop.coverage --json` reports the discovered set + per-directory undiscovered counts — the data contract for the step-2 extension that paints the mask.
briefing.if_rejected: The whole-tree viewport stays a forest/workspace read with no per-file discovery signal; bdo cannot see which files agents have never opened, only which were edited (native VS Code).
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and reversible: a pure-stdlib PostToolUse sensor that writes one gitignored telemetry line per file-tool use (fail-open, exit 0 always — it can never gate a tool) and a read-only fold that writes nothing. Reverting is removing one hook line, one register entry, and two files; no truth moves, no write path on the append-only log.
briefing.mechanism: file_touch.py reads the PostToolUse stdin, maps Read->read / Edit|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit->edit, extracts the concrete file_path (notebook_path for NotebookEdit), normalizes it repo-relative when under the repo, and appends {ts, session, action, path} to the gitignored sink — failing open on any error. It is wired in settings.json (matcher Read|Edit|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit) and declared in activity-register.json (the teeth refuse an undeclared collector). loop/coverage.py senses tracked files via `git ls-files`, folds the touch log into the discovered set, and crosses them: a tracked file never touched reads undiscovered, a touched one reads discovered. The §10 teeth are the pure classifiers, proven non-vacuous in tests/test_coverage.py (a constant 'all discovered' is caught). edited/red is NOT computed (native VS Code).

source_artifacts: bdo 2026-06-22: the per-file mask — which files agents have NOT discovered (undiscovered), plus edited/red (native VS Code)
